text: Hi, this is Dr. Vishal Soni. I'm a consultant laparoscopic surgeon at Zydus Hospital, Ahmedabad. And today we'll be seeing a laparoscopic appendectomy in a case of acute appendicitis.

The optical trocar is visible, with two 10 mm trocars and one 5 mm walking port used for the procedure. The inflamed appendix is identified, and the surgeon uses a ligature in the right hand to perform serial division of the mesoappendix, devascularizing the appendix. The appendicular artery is carefully managed, and the mesoappendix is divided close to the appendix, making it lean for easier extraction. Dissection continues at the appendicular base, where inflammation is noted in the body and base region.

The surgeon performs adhesiolysis on the lateral attachments of the appendix, revealing the thickened and inflamed peritoneum. The appendicular base is clearly visible as it continues into the cecum. Two endo loops are placed on the patient side, and the appendix is cut without using a third loop due to minimal inflammation.

The surgeon cauterizes the mucosa of the appendix stump, then extracts the appendix specimen through the umbilical port. A thorough inspection of the abdomen and terminal ileum is conducted, followed by repositioning the omentum near the base of the appendix.
